instruction:
Consider the set $\{1,2,\dots,g\}$ and all its nonempty subsets, which we'll denote as $G_1, G_2, \dots, G_{2^g-1}$ in any arbitrary order. Now, construct a matrix $S$ of size $(2^g-1) \times (2^g-1)$ where each element $m_{ij}$ is defined as follows: \[ m_{ij} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if $G_i$ and $G_j$ have at least one element in common,} \\ 0 & \text{if $G_i$ and $G_j$ are disjoint.} \end{cases} \]Your task is to evaluate the determinant of $S$. In case multiple determinant values are possible, compute the sum of all distinct determinant values.

Options:
A. -4
B. 1
C. 5
D. 0
E. 4
F. 3
G. 2
H. -1
I. -3
J. -2
